name: teams-notification-handler
description: Processes Teams notifications that have been forwarded to email. Extracts the notification content, summarizes it, and can take automated actions.

parameters.name: emailSubject
parameters.description: The subject line of the incoming email

parameters.name: emailBody
parameters.description: The body content of the incoming email

parameters.name: emailReceivedTime
parameters.description: When the email was received (ISO 8601 format)

steps.name: parse-notification
steps.systemPrompt: You are an assistant that parses Microsoft Teams email notifications. Extract the key information from the notification.

steps.name: categorize-and-prioritize
steps.systemPrompt: You are an assistant that categorizes and prioritizes notifications. Based on the parsed notification, determine its urgency and category.
steps.userPrompt:
Based on the parsed notification, determine:
1) Category: [urgent-action | meeting-reminder | information | social | other]
2) Priority: [high | medium | low]
3) Requires response: [yes | no]
4) Brief reason for categorization

Output as JSON with keys: category, priority, requiresResponse, reason
steps.inputHandlerPrompt: Take the parsed notification details from the previous step.

steps.name: generate-summary
steps.systemPrompt: You are an assistant that creates concise summaries of notifications for quick review.
steps.userPrompt:
Create a brief, actionable summary of this Teams notification. Include:
- One-line summary
- Key person(s) involved
- Required action (if any)
- Deadline (if mentioned)

Format as a clean, readable summary.
steps.inputHandlerPrompt: Combine the parsed notification content with the categorization from the previous steps.
steps.outputHandlerPrompt: Ensure the summary is concise (under 100 words) and actionable.
